Share a Vue CLI 2 Dev Server Over Your Local Network

When you're developing locally, you sometimes need to share your dev server with someone else. In Vue CLI 2 there's a small config tweak you need to make — otherwise other people on your local network can't reach your site.
// can be overwritten by process.env.PORT, if port is in use, a free one will be determined
You only need to change one thing —
That's it. Now your Vue CLI 2 dev server is shareable over the LAN.

working and can share with others.
If it still doesn't work, try these:
Make sure the firewall and router allow the connection through.
The user trying to connect needs to be on the same network as you — same Wi-Fi, basically.
You're giving out the wrong IP. Use the IPv4 one — mine for example is

— your IPv4 address is in there.
